headline: Sandboxing local AI agents on macOS
lede: As local AI agents gain deeper access to personal files, the boundary between assistant and intruder thins. Agent Safehouse leverages macOS kernel sandboxing to enforce strict permission boundaries.

headline: Seeing the analog pits of a LaserDisc
lede: Through the lens of a high-powered microscope, the physical structure of a LaserDisc reveals its secret: an vast, analog landscape of data. It is a striking reminder of how we once etched light and sound into solid matter.

headline: An ultra-compact devboard for tight spaces
lede: Measuring less than a centimeter square, this tiny Attiny1616 board proves that powerful hardware need not be bulky. It is an exercise in minimalist design for the most constrained PCB environments.

headline: Reviving literate programming in the age of agents
lede: The historical friction of literate programming has long discouraged its adoption, as keeping prose and logic in sync was often a manual chore. AI automation finally offers a path to bridge this gap, ensuring documentation and code never drift apart again.
pullquote: The agent era transforms the labor of documentation into a collaborative partnership with the machine

headline: RSS emerges from the shadow of walled gardens
lede: As algorithmic fatigue settles in, the open web’s oldest syndication standard is finding renewed purpose. Users are reclaiming their feeds, trading centralized curation for the quiet agency of the reader.

headline: A comparative analysis of 2025 single-board computers
lede: The market for single-board computers has fractured under the pressure of fluctuating silicon costs and supply chain volatility. By analyzing fifteen distinct platforms, we can categorize the shifting landscape of high-performance embedded computing.

headline: March 2026: What are you building?
lede: From home automation experiments to clever CLI utilities, the monthly progress report offers a vibrant snapshot of the hacker collective. It is a reminder that the best projects often begin as a personal curiosity.

headline: A custom lightbox implementation for modern browsers
lede: FrameBook provides a masterclass in clean, lightweight UI engineering, focusing on the mechanics of image presentation without the bloat. It explores the interplay of transitions and gestures that define a modern lightbox.

headline: Writing to read-only memory via procfs
lede: Linux kernel interfaces often contain obscure paths that permit direct memory manipulation, bypassing standard hardware protections. This mechanism illustrates how the kernel prioritizes its own administrative access over traditional page permissions.

headline: Consolidating services into a unified homelab
lede: Self-hosting demands a balance between feature density and maintenance overhead. By utilizing TrueNAS and containerized orchestration, local infrastructure becomes a manageable, resilient asset.
